Think: The user wants exercises for strong arms. "Strong arms" implies working both biceps and triceps. I have four exercises available: two for biceps and two for triceps. To provide a comprehensive recommendation for "strong arms," I should suggest at least one exercise for each muscle group. Given the user's preference for 'concise' and 'in a table', I will present the exercises in a table format, briefly explaining their benefits and how to perform them. I will also include images for clarity. I will select one bicep and one tricep exercise. Dumbbell Biceps Curl and Dumbbells Seated Triceps Extension are good foundational choices.
Textual response:Building strong arms involves working both your biceps and triceps. Here are two effective exercises, presented concisely in a table, that target these key muscle groups:

| Exercise Name | Target Muscle | Benefits | How to Perform (Key Points) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Dumbbell Biceps Curl** | Biceps | Builds muscle mass and strength in the front of your upper arm. | Stand or sit, hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Keep elbows close to your body. Curl the weights up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Slowly lower back down. |
| **Dumbbells Seated Triceps Extension** | Triceps | Strengthens the back of your upper arm, contributing to overall arm size and pushing strength. | Sit on a bench, hold one dumbbell with both hands above your head. Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbows. Extend your arms to push the dumbbell back up to the starting position. |
